calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-04-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Wade appeared by zoom for and with Denise Wade by zoom.
Ruth Sullivan appeared by zoom for and with Lorae Rose (Objector) by zoom.

The Court stated that it reviewed the materials, that it would like to get this wrapped up to get the Beneficiaries paid and asked the Parties how they want to proceed. The Court also informed the Parties that it is looking at a reduction of the statutory fees in the amount of $3,900,00 and a surcharge $6,750.00 which would go back into the estate and reduce Denise's share by $3,375.00, and asked if the Parties wanted to add anything, or submit on the matter. Ms. Sullivan stated that she submits on the papers. Mr. Wade addressed the issue of the $3,900.00 reduction of the statutory fees stating that his Client incurred expenses out of her own pocket and that he believes his Client should not be charged those fees. The Amended Final Report and Petition for Approval of Account, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (183 Rey Street San Francisco, Ca 94134)
Off Calendar Nov-22-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Wade for and with Denise Wade.
Lorae Rose in Propria Persona.
Bailey Cheung (Realtor for the buyers).
Cui Yu Lin, Han Zhou Ye and Xing Xiu Liang (buyers) also present.

The Court stated that it has no confirmation that the Buyers got individual notice. Mr. Wade indicated that the three buyers that are present as well as Han Zhong Ye (the 4th Buyer who is not present) got notice of the sale. The Court considers the three present buyers having waived notice. The sale was confirmed at $938,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed October 17, 2019. The Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property is under submission subject to receiving a proposed order reflecting the financial institutions and receiving a waiver of notice from the fourth buyer. The sale proceeds are to be deposited into separate insured blocked accounts in lieu of bond. Status on filing of the receipts for the blocked accounts is set for December 16, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Aug-28-2019 Continued To Sep-25-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ruth Sullivan for and with Lorae Rose (Beneficiary/Heir-at-Law).
David Wade appeared by telephone for Denise Rose.

The Court stated that it is looking for a petition for final distribution. Mr. Wade stated that the next step is to get the house sold, that his Client has limited powers, that publication is taking place today and that they have a buyer that wants the property. Mr. Wade also stated that they have a confirmed offer and that they can close on the sale in 60-days. Ms. Sullivan stated that the Administrator has been living in the house since the Decedent died, that she requested a year ago to Counsel to sell the house, that the Administrator indicated to someone that she will be living there for three years and that the Administrator needs close monitoring/supervision. Status on close of estate is continued to September 25, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court advised Counsel that if he is not prepared to file a petition for final distribution by then, he will need to file a petition for approval of report on status of administration that complies with all of the requirements of the probate code no later than September 18, 2019.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-05-2017 Continued To Jan-03-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Denise Rose in Propria Persona.
Lorae Rose in Propria Persona.

Lorae Rose stated that she is not here to object and that she just wants to be kept informed as to the Estate. The Court explained that administration of an Estate is complicated and recommends hiring an attorney. Denise Rose stated that the initial petition was by her only and that since then she has hired someone to assist her. The Court explained that she can not take legal advice from the document preparer. Denise Rose also stated that the value of the estate is $500,000.00, that the property is owned by her Mother, that she needs to let the creditors know and that there is only one mortgage for $31,000.00. In addition, Denise Rose stated that she is afraid of not being able to afford an Attorney, that she is concerned that at the end of probate they will have to sell the property and that she is living there. The Court echoed that they may have to sell the property, and stated that the Siblings are entitled to their right to their share. Lorae stated that she obtained Ruth Sullivan on September 20th and that her Sister didn't want her. Denise stated that she wanted to be Co-Administrator. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to January 3, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Denise Rose to look into getting an Attorney.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
